title: Keep secrets and heavy computation off-chain
description: Place only what must be trustlessly verified on-chain; never store private data on-chain and move expensive logic off-chain with on-chain verification.
body: Use when deciding what data and computation belong on-chain. Do not store passwords, keys, or private data on-chain (all state is publicly readable even if not exposed by an interface), and avoid expensive computation that wastes gas. Actions: store only state that needs consensus/verification on-chain, keep secrets and bulk data off-chain (with on-chain hashes/commitments), and move heavy computation off-chain with an on-chain proof or signature check. Validation signals include a review confirming no sensitive value is written to storage and gas costs reflect minimal on-chain work. Risk boundary: 'private' on-chain storage is fully visible to anyone reading the chain; treat any secret written on-chain as disclosed and a critical privacy defect.
